title: A two-cell P0 projection satisfies a Pythagorean energy identity

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The two-cell squared L2 energy splits into the squared energy of the constant projection plus the squared residual energy.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real cell values
the P0 projection is the two-cell average on both cells
the squared L2 energy is the sum of the two squared coordinates
informal.expected_conclusion: The original squared energy equals projected squared energy plus residual squared energy.

declarations.name: cellL2Sq2
declarations.statement: def cellL2Sq2 (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 ^ 2 + u1 ^ 2

declarations.name: p0ProjectionResidualSq2
declarations.statement: def p0ProjectionResidualSq2 (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := (u0 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) ^ 2 + (u1 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) ^ 2

declarations.name: p0Projection2_pythagorean
declarations.statement: theorem p0Projection2_pythagorean (u0 u1 : Real) : cellL2Sq2 u0 u1 = cellL2Sq2 (p0Projection2Left u0 u1) (p0Projection2Right u0 u1) + p0ProjectionResidualSq2 u0 u1

tasks.input: Translate the Pythagorean energy identity for a two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem p0Projection2_pythagorean (u0 u1 : Real) : cellL2Sq2 u0 u1 = cellL2Sq2 (p0Projection2Left u0 u1) (p0Projection2Right u0 u1) + p0ProjectionResidualSq2 u0 u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the squared-energy, projected-coordinate, residual, and average definitions.
tasks.target: Unfold cellL2Sq2, p0Projection2Left, p0Projection2Right, p0ProjectionResidualSq2, and cellAverage2, then use ring.
